title: Today's Tech Brief: Grand Theft Auto VI is warping the video game release calendar
summary: A look at how Grand Theft Auto VI is impacting the video game release calendar and other tech news.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to Today's Tech Brief. I've got a couple of interesting stories to share with you today, starting with the gaming world. Grand Theft Auto VI, one of the most anticipated games in recent memory, is set to drop in November. The question is, what's happening to the video game release calendar with this behemoth taking center stage?

According to a report from The Verge, the months leading up to GTA VI's release are getting increasingly crowded. It seems like every other major game is trying to squeeze out a launch before the GTA juggernaut takes over. I'm looking at you, Summer Game Fest - it's like the gaming world's version of a pre-Olympic warm-up event. The real question is, can any other game compete with the hype surrounding GTA VI?

In other news, Congress is still trying to figure out what to do about warrantless surveillance. It appears that Trump's appointment of Bill Pulte as director of national intelligence has thrown a wrench into Republicans' plans to reauthorize Section 702. My read is that this is going to be a tricky situation to navigate, with many questions about security, privacy, and the role of government in the digital age. And if you're wondering why this matters, think about it like this: it's a balancing act between keeping the country safe and keeping citizens' data private.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.

sources.title: Grand Theft Auto VI is warping the video game release calendar
Congress still can’t decide what to do about warrantless surveillance
